tasks.title: Create env-registry.ts with EnvVarDefinition interface and all HOSPEDA_* shared vars
tasks.description:
Create `packages/config/src/env-registry.ts` with the `EnvVarDefinition` interface (name, description, type, required, secret, defaultValue, exampleValue, enumValues, apps, category) and define ALL `HOSPEDA_*` vars from Appendix A (~35 vars including DATABASE_URL, BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, MERCADO_PAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN, RESEND_API_KEY, CRON_SECRET, SENTRY_DSN, etc.). Each var must have complete metadata. Export as `ENV_REGISTRY` readonly array.

Files: `packages/config/src/env-registry.ts` (create)

Tests: Unit test in `packages/config/test/env-registry.test.ts` verifying all HOSPEDA_* vars are present with required fields populated.

subtasks.title: Define EnvVarDefinition interface with all fields
Add all HOSPEDA_* vars from Appendix A (~35 entries)

tasks.title: Add API_*, PUBLIC_*, VITE_*, Docker, and System vars to env-registry
tasks.description:
Extend `ENV_REGISTRY` in `packages/config/src/env-registry.ts` with: all `API_*` tuning vars (~25: CORS, CACHE, COMPRESSION, RATE_LIMIT, SECURITY, VALIDATION, RESPONSE, METRICS, LOG, PORT, HOST), all `PUBLIC_*` vars (4: API_URL, SITE_URL, SENTRY_DSN, SENTRY_RELEASE), all `VITE_*` vars (~17 from Appendix A), Docker vars (5: POSTGRES_USER/PASSWORD/DB/PORT, REDIS_PORT), and System vars (5: NODE_ENV, CI, VERCEL, VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA, SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT).

Files: `packages/config/src/env-registry.ts` (modify)

Tests: Extend test to verify all categories are represented.

subtasks.title: Add all API_* tuning vars (~25 entries)
Add PUBLIC_* and VITE_* client vars

tasks.description:

Files: `packages/config/src/index.ts` (modify)

Tests: Verify import works in existing config tests.

subtasks.title: Add export for ENV_REGISTRY and EnvVarDefinition

tasks.title: Write comprehensive unit tests for env-registry
tasks.description:
Create `packages/config/test/env-registry.test.ts` with tests:
- No duplicate var names in registry
- All entries have non-empty description
- All entries have valid type ('string'|'url'|'number'|'boolean'|'enum')
- All entries with type 'enum' have non-empty enumValues
- All entries have at least one app
- All entries have non-empty exampleValue
- Secret vars have required=true or are explicitly optional
- Category is non-empty for all entries
- Snapshot test of var count per category

Files: `packages/config/test/env-registry.test.ts` (create)

Tests: This IS the test task. Run with `pnpm --filter @repo/config test`.

subtasks.title: Create test file with all validation assertions
Add snapshot test for var count per category

tasks.title: Create scripts/env/pull.ts - interactive Vercel env pull
tasks.description:
Create `scripts/env/pull.ts` implementing the full pull flow from US-06:
1. Prompt for app and Vercel environment
2. Use VercelApiClient to fetch env vars
3. For each var: show name, current local value (from .env.local), Vercel value, description
4. Ask for confirmation before writing each var
5. Write approved vars to app's .env.local
6. Show summary of changes at the end
7. Never overwrite without confirmation

Files: `scripts/env/pull.ts` (create)

Tests: Manual test by pulling from Vercel.

tasks.title: Create scripts/env/push.ts - interactive Vercel env push
tasks.description:
Create `scripts/env/push.ts` implementing the full push flow from US-06:
1. Prompt for app and Vercel environment
2. Read app's .env.local
3. Compare with Vercel vars via API
4. For each new/changed var: show name, local value, Vercel value, description
5. Ask for confirmation before pushing each var
6. Create/update vars in Vercel via API
7. Show summary of changes

Files: `scripts/env/push.ts` (create)

Tests: Manual test by pushing a test var to Vercel.

subtasks.title: Push approved vars via Vercel API

tasks.title: Create scripts/env/check.ts - env var verification/audit
tasks.description:
Create `scripts/env/check.ts` implementing the check flow from US-06:
1. For each app, compare .env.example vars vs Vercel vars (all environments)
2. Report vars in .env.example but missing in Vercel (by environment)
3. Report vars in Vercel but not in .env.example (potentially obsolete)
4. Report vars with different values between environments
5. Support --ci flag for non-interactive mode
6. Exit code 0 if all required vars present, 1 otherwise

Files: `scripts/env/check.ts` (create)

Tests: Unit tests for check logic with mocked Vercel API responses.

subtasks.title: Compare with Vercel vars per environment

4. **Web client vars table**: All PUBLIC_* vars
5. **Admin client vars table**: All VITE_* vars

Content must match env-registry exactly.

5. Verify each app starts: `pnpm dev:api`, `pnpm dev:web`, `pnpm dev:admin`
